title: To Mr. Cyriack Skinner upon his Blindness.
content:
Cyriack, this three years day these eys, though clear
    To outward view, of blemish or of spot;
    Bereft of light thir seeing have forgot,
    Nor to thir idle orbs doth sight appear
    Of Sun or Moon or Starre throughout the year,
    Or man or woman. Yet I argue not
    Against heavns hand or will, nor bate a jot
    Of heart or hope; but still bear vp and steer
    Right onward. What supports me, dost thou ask?
    The conscience, Friend, to have lost them overply'd
    In libertyes defence, my noble task,
    Of which all Europe talks from side to side.
    This thought might lead me through the world's vain mask
    Content though blind, had I no better guide.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, John Milton delivers a powerful performance in "To Mr. Cyriack Skinner upon his Blindness."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
